summary: End-to-end testing of LeaseFlow (FastAPI + React + MongoDB). Backend: 34/34 pytest tests passed covering auth, agents CRUD, leads CRUD/CSV, stats, setup wizard, sheets config, demo seed/clear, webhook logs, and the public Vapi webhook (lead branch + Vapi event branch + auth modes + content-type + body cap + idempotency). Frontend: all 8 routes (Dashboard, Pipeline, Leads, Live Calls, Vapi Setup, Setup Guide, Webhook Logs, Settings) render correctly with no console errors after admin login; KPI cards, 7-day bar chart, status breakdown, kanban with 5 columns, leads table, webhook URL/secret copy fields, and Settings sub-cards (Google Sheets / Team / Demo data) all visible and populated after demo seed.

issues: Login page renders with form pinned to bottom-left of viewport (lots of whitespace at top); right pane image+copy is fine. Cosmetic only — submit still works.

action_items: Optional: tidy Login page vertical layout so the form is centered (currently the SIGN IN block sits at the very bottom of the viewport with a large empty area above)

Optional: residual leads named 'Test Caller'/'Test Lead' from prior smoke runs are still in the DB (source=Manual). Not created by this iteration. Either delete them manually or filter out non-demo TEST data.
critical_code_review_comments: vapi_webhook.py L231-233: precedence in `request.client.host if request.client else 'unknown'` — due to Python's `or` precedence the chain works, but parenthesizing the conditional would be clearer.
server.py uses deprecated `@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown')` — FastAPI recommends lifespan context managers (works fine today, deprecation warning only).
/api/setup/test-webhook posts to `http://127.0.0.1:8001/api/public/vapi-webhook` (hard-coded). Acceptable for a self-test but a configurable internal URL would be cleaner.
/api/auth/team is exposed to all authenticated users (members can list owners). Consider `require_owner` or scrubbing emails for non-owners.
PATCH /api/agents and DELETE /api/leads have no owner-only guard — any authenticated member can rename agents or delete any lead. Tighten if multi-tenant.
demo_clear matches by activity message string ('Demo lead seeded') which is brittle — better to tag demo activity with a flag field.
Lead `phone` regex on webhook accepts dots and spaces but pipeline auto-link strips non-digits — fine, but document the canonicalization.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (8/8 routes load, all key UI elements present, no console errors)

test_credentials: admin@form.rentals / leaseflow2026 (verified working). Global webhook secret env-loaded and accepted. Agent secrets auto-generated on /api/agents POST.
seed_data_creation: Created 8 demo leads via /api/demo/seed during testing and cleared them via /api/demo/clear. Test agents created during pytest are auto-deleted by the session-scoped fixture teardown. Note: a few leftover 'Test Caller'/'Test Lead' leads from PRIOR smoke testing remain in the DB (Manual source) — not from this iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_leaseflow.py for regression. It is fully self-contained with session-scoped admin token + auto-cleanup of test agents and TEST_-prefixed leads. The webhook tests exercise all auth failure modes, content-type 415, body 413, idempotency replay, and Vapi event upserts. WebSocket /api/ws connects (LIVE indicator visible in sidebar). Login lands on '/' which the React router treats as dashboard.
rca of the issue: No bugs found requiring RCA. All listed feature checks behave per spec.
